1	<!--
2	Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
3	All rights reserved.
4	SPDX-License-Identifier: LicenseRef-Proprietary
5	-->
6	
7	# Allocation Coding Guide (ACG) — Reference the Home
8	
9	## Purpose
10	
11	ACG codifies one discipline: **don't recreate inline what already has a named
12	home — reference the home.** It is the language-neutral, allocation-facing
13	sibling to BCG (host bash), RCG (host Rust), WSG (Windows transport), and CBG
14	(cloud step). Those govern *how* code in one environment is written; ACG governs
15	*where a fact or a name is allowed to live*, and what source must do when a home
16	already exists: point at it, never restate it.
17	
18	ACG is the **source-side complement to MCM**. MCM builds the named homes — quoins
19	in specs, constants in code; ACG governs source's obligation to reference rather
20	than recreate them. That complement is why ACG lives in the CMK kit beside MCM
21	and AXLA rather than in any single-language kit.
22	
23	ACG is **v1**: it blesses and names emergent practice and states the spine. It
24	does **not** mandate universals ("every verb must announce"); those are
25	candidates, confirmed move-by-move, not law. There is no qualify-time enforcement
26	in v1. Every move-type below is licensed precisely — most are detect-and-report,
27	not repair.
28	
29	## How this document is organized — frame, then catalog
30	
31	ACG separates two kinds of knowledge, because they earn their form differently:
32	
33	- **The Frame** (prose, no IDs) — the spine, the three homes, the v1 posture, the
34	  move discipline. Systematic and interderivable; internalized, not cited by
35	  number.
36	- **The Move Catalog** (numbered `ACGm_`) — discrete, verifier-backed move-types,
37	  each of which something *points at*: a constant's breadcrumb comment, a
38	  detect-and-report verb, a conformance-engine row, a review flagging a
39	  violation. An ID earns its existence only when a citer will exist.
40	
41	This split is itself the load-bearing test (BCG Core Philosophy): catalog the
42	discrete moves; systematize the discipline behind them.
43	
44	## The spine — reference the home
45	
46	The governing idea, stated once:
47	
48	> Don't recreate inline what has a named home. **Values → constants; concepts →
49	> quoin-refs; statements → definition sites.** MCM builds the homes; ACG governs
50	> source's duty to reference rather than recreate.
51	
52	This is the **allocation instance of Load-Bearing Complexity** (CLAUDE.md). A
53	recreated value or name is a non-load-bearing duplicate: its existence adds drift
54	risk without adding correctness, and the drift it invites is exactly the gap
55	between intent and behavior the principle forbids. BCG's and RCG's Constant
56	Discipline and Interface Contamination are the per-language instances of this;
57	ACG is the general statement those instantiate, plus the one thing a
58	single-language guide cannot state — *where prose with no code-home actually
59	goes.*
60	
61	## The three homes — allocate by when-read
62	
63	A fact has exactly one right home, fixed by *when it is read*:
64	
65	| When read | Home | Carries |
66	|-----------|------|---------|
67	| **Design-time** | the spec (MCM quoin) | what a thing *is* and why it is shaped so — the conceptual model |
68	| **Edit-time** | the source comment | operational mechanics only — language idiom, and the Palisade (foreign-boundary) characterization a maintainer needs *at the code* |
69	| **Execution-time** | the runtime announcement | what is happening now — intent printed as the operation runs |
70	| **History-time** | the commit | when and why a thing changed — git log and blame own it; version narration in the document body is a tidemark (MCM `mcm_tidemark`) |
71	
72	Most conceptual comments are **temporal misallocation**: design-time or
73	execution-time knowledge dumped into the edit-time medium, where it rots for lack
74	of a forcing function. The spec is re-read when the design is questioned; the
75	announcement is re-read every run; an edit-time comment restating either is read
76	by no one with a reason to correct it when it drifts.
77	
78	Two edit-time/execution-time source-doc forms are distinct and **both blessed**:
79	
80	- **Contract header** — a bounded edit-time comment stating a function's input
81	  contract and invariants. Read when editing the function.
82	- **Intent announcement** — an execution-time printout naming what the operation
83	  is doing. Read when running it.
84	
85	Failure-path option disclosure (the missing-parameter-shows-the-options
86	practice, live in `rbfc_require_vessel_sigil`) is blessed alongside happy-path
87	announcement: an error that lists the valid forms is an execution-time
88	announcement of the contract, not contamination.
89	
90	**The execution-time home is plural off the terminal.** "Announcement" reads as
91	a single printed stream because a CLI gives you one. A routine whose product
92	does not go to a terminal — an MCP command, a library call, a daemon — splits
93	it: the **product** (the value returned to the caller), the **trace**
94	(diagnostic breadcrumbs), and the **error** travel different media, bound per
95	project. Naming the full output-role roster is future work; one invariant earns
96	its place now: **trace has no re-read-every-run forcing function, so it rots like
97	an edit-time comment — never make it the sole home of design-time knowledge.**
98	
99	## The third clause — statements → definition sites
100	
101	The spine governs prose, not only values and names. Every normative
102	idea — a rule, a scope claim, a constraint, a rationale — has exactly one
103	authoritative wording at one definition site. Every other appearance is a
104	**citation**: it may name the home and orient the reader; it may not restate
105	the content in other words.
106	
107	A paraphrase is a fork — a second wording that drifts independently, leaving a
108	reader unable to know which wording is law. A **word cancer** is the metastatic
109	form: variations of one idea scattered across documents, each restatement
110	enlarging the drift surface, none authoritative. The term is deliberately ugly.
111	The pattern is the most recurrent failure mode of LLM-assisted maintenance of
112	large codebases: a model completing "what should be said here" recreates a
113	nearby idea in fresh words, and every recreation seeds the next.
114	
115	For an LLM reader the cost is mechanical, not aesthetic: two near-paraphrases
116	are nearly the same retrieval key, and once they drift the model retrieves a
117	confident *blend* — statement-level interference, the prose analogue of the
118	prefix-collision mechanism in
119	`Memos/memo-20260610-quoin-minting-introspection.md`. A quoin gives a concept
120	one string; this clause gives a claim one wording. Canonical specimen: the
121	2026-06-10 Diff-Friendly-Prose repair, where a context file *characterized* a
122	spec's scope instead of citing it, and the characterization carried the error
123	for months.
124	
125	**Normative register** rides this clause on the spec side. In a spec, every
126	sentence either constrains — an implementer or validator could act differently
127	because it exists — or is explicitly marked as rationale or gestalt (MCM's task
128	lens `**Gestalt**:` field is the blessed form: metaphor marked as interpretive
129	guidance). Unmarked ornament is contamination: it spends reader attention and
130	implies constraint where none exists. A code comment restating design-time
131	knowledge is the same failure in the other medium (temporal misallocation,
132	above); flowery spec prose is its mirror.
133	
134	## The fourth clause — wire formats are named homes
135	
136	A JSON document this project authors on **both sides** — an envelope, a result
137	slot, a substitutions blob — is itself a home: a wire format with senders and
138	receivers that must stay findable from each other. Its keys are therefore
139	**minted names** (CLAUDE.md mint: any persistent name anywhere is in scope),
140	carrying one key-sprue per format: `rblv_digest`, never bare `digest`. The sprue
141	is what makes the format greppable — `grep rblv_` returns the author, every
142	consumer, and the schema definition, with no central registry to drift (the
143	`buo` tweak-sprue precedent; the `_RBGL_` substitution registers are the senior
144	worked example). One sprue per *format*, not per file or per domain: two formats
145	sharing a sprue blur the census that is the sprue's whole purpose.
146	
147	The boundary is the Palisade: JSON conforming to a **foreign** schema — a Cloud
148	Build envelope, an OCI manifest, a vendor API response — keeps the foreign keys.
149	Sprued keys inside a foreign document are the tell that someone is decorating a
150	schema they don't own.
151	
152	The clause is prospective: it binds every wire format as it is authored or
153	touched; no retroactive sweep is mandated. The conversion move is catalogued as
154	ACGm_108.
155	
156	## The move discipline — one litmus, three rules
157	
158	Every cleanup move obeys one litmus:
159	
160	> **Mutate only where a *wrong* move is cheaply caught; everywhere else the verb
161	> is detect-and-report, never repair.**
162	
163	Code-side moves — the value resolves through a named constant, and shellcheck
164	plus the fast suite verify it — may mutate now. Spec- and document-side moves are
165	detect-and-report until the lexer/linter that would catch a wrong move exists.
166	
167	Three rules ride that litmus:
168	
169	1. **Triage is part of every move, not an afterthought.** Exclude the
170	   false-positive class first; declare the authoritative side before any
171	   alignment; gate scope to settled lanes — never terms a hot heat is still
172	   moving. A sweep run against vocabulary two heats are still relocating only
173	   re-creates the drift it meant to kill.
174	2. **A checker proves itself against a known answer before its output is
175	   trusted.** The conformance fixture (next section) is the canonical home for
176	   such checks — ACG eating its own dog food: a named home, referenced not
177	   recreated.
178	3. **Concept linkage is name-identity.** The implementing symbol *is* the link: a
179	   function name equals its quoin's display-text, a tabtarget filename is the
180	   quoin's sprue, a scoped method is its inlay. A source-to-spec comment is the
181	   *exception*, reserved for where no single symbol can carry the link.
182	
183	## The named home — the conformance fixture
184	
185	Rule 2 and move-types ACGm_102/103 all defer to one concrete home: the
186	**`conformance`** fast-tier theurge fixture
187	(`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrn_conformance.rs`), registered in the `fast` suite. It
188	is to evicted vocabulary what BUK's `bug_require_clean_tree` is to the clean-tree
189	gate — the single named place the check lives, so no caller improvises its own.
190	
191	The fixture is a data-driven engine, not a word list. It reads rows of
192	`{kill_stem, keep_contexts}` and scans `Tools/` and `tt/` with identifier-boundary
193	awareness, flagging a stem as a bare token while sparing it inside a kept
194	identifier (`Identifier`) or under an exempt path (`PathPrefix`). A row with empty
195	`keep_contexts` is a *pure corpse* — the term must appear nowhere. The engine
196	proves itself against known inputs before its verdict on the live tree is trusted
197	(move discipline, rule 2), so it ships proven with zero production rows.
198	
199	**The rule: retire a term → add a row there. Never improvise a grep.** Population
200	is deliberately deferred — each term gains its row behind its own cutover, owned
201	by the heat retiring it — never against vocabulary a hot heat is still moving
202	(move discipline, rule 1).
203	
204	## The Move Catalog
205	
206	Each move-type states five things: **Detect** (the rule that finds a site),
207	**Authority** (the ground truth a site is judged against), **Licensing**,
208	**Verifier**, and a countable **Done**. Headers tag the licensing: **🔧
209	mutate-now** (a wrong move is cheaply caught — repair in place) or **🔍
210	detect-only** (read and report; no repair until a verifier exists). Numbered from
211	101 to leave room for insertion; once a move has a citer it is never renumbered.
212	
213	### 🔧 ACGm_101: magic-string → constant
214	
215	- **Detect:** a literal value — a path fragment, a filename, a repeated number or
216	  string — that *constructs or addresses* something, where a named home exists or
217	  should. Not human-facing prose.
218	- **Authority:** the value resolves through a single named constant home (e.g.
219	  the `RBCC_rbr*_file` family in `rbcc_Constants.sh`). The constant is ground
220	  truth; every literal is a copy that can drift.
221	- **Licensing:** mutate-now — the constant either resolves or the verifier fails,
222	  so a wrong move dies loud and immediately.
223	- **Verifier:** bash — `tt/rbw-tl.Shellcheck.sh` + `tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.fast.sh`
224	  green; Rust — `tt/rbw-tb.Build.sh` + `tt/rbw-tt.Test.sh` green.
225	- **Done:** every genuine construction site resolves through the constant; the
226	  constant carries a breadcrumb to its spec quoin.
227	- **Triage:** naming a file *for a human* in a message is not a magic string —
228	  `doc_params`, `buc_info`/`buh_line` messages, and comments keep the literal.
229	  Exclude that class before sweeping.
230	- **Worked example:** the `rbrv.env` filename sweep is the first worked
231	  application of this move.
232	
233	### 🔍 ACGm_102: name-identity
234	
235	- **Detect:** an operation quoin (voiced `//axvo_procedure` or `//axvo_method`)
236	  whose display-text and implementing symbol disagree.
237	- **Authority:** the implementing symbol is the link (move discipline, rule 3) —
238	  a procedure's function name equals its quoin's display-text; a source-to-spec
239	  comment is the exception where no single symbol can carry it.
240	- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side; no mutation until the lexer/linter
241	  exists.
242	- **Verifier:** none mechanized in v1 — read and reported by hand. The
243	  conformance fixture is the future verifier.
244	- **Done:** a report of operation-quoin / symbol disagreements; nothing mutated.
245	- **Formal dependency:** full mechanical decidability waits on an AXLA arity
246	  change — an explicit symbol lookahead on `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method`
247	  (one `mcm_inlay`, the prefix-disciplined source symbol; mirroring
248	  `axvo_tabtarget`'s literal lookahead). That change carries a migration across
249	  every live operation voicing, so it is deferred to its own pace (see *The
250	  AXLA/MCM interface*). Until it lands, the move is read against the existing
251	  `axvo_*` voicings — which already mark *which* quoins are operations — plus the
252	  implementation symbol named at an operation's detail site (`axhems_scoped_method`).
253	
254	### 🔍 ACGm_103: typed-parameter lookahead
255	
256	- **Detect:** a parameter whose type is resolvable by bounded lookahead but is
257	  not yet carried as a type quoin-ref.
258	- **Authority:** the lookahead scanning rule (below) — the parameter's type is
259	  the attribute-reference the marker reads.
260	- **Licensing:** detect-only — document-side.
261	- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter.
262	- **Done:** a report of parameters whose type is derivable but unhomed; nothing
263	  mutated.
264	
265	### 🔍 ACGm_104: conceptual-comment eviction
266	
267	- **Detect:** an edit-time comment carrying design-time knowledge — what a thing
268	  *is*, why it is shaped so, how it relates to other concepts — rather than
269	  operational mechanics or Palisade characterization.
270	- **Authority:** the three-homes table. The spec quoin is the design-time home;
271	  where no quoin exists, the move creates or extends one, then cites it.
272	- **Licensing:** detect-only for the sweep — relocation mutates two documents
273	  (source and spec) and a wrong move is not cheaply caught, so each relocation
274	  lands as its own reviewed move, never batch-applied.
275	- **Verifier:** none in v1; the future lexer/linter plus spec link validation.
276	- **Done:** a report of misallocated comments, each tagged with its receiving
277	  quoin (existing or proposed); relocations land one reviewed move at a time.
278	
279	### 🔍 ACGm_105: paraphrase collapse
280	
281	- **Detect:** the same normative idea worded differently in two or more places —
282	  across specs, context files, handbooks, or comments. The smell: a reader could
283	  ask "which of these is law?"
284	- **Authority:** the statements → definition sites clause. Triage declares one
285	  home authoritative; every other site becomes a citation.
286	- **Licensing:** detect-only — the collapse mutates prose in several documents
287	  at once and judgment selects the surviving wording; never batch-applied.
288	- **Verifier:** none mechanized; recurrence is caught by re-running the detect
289	  sweep against the declared home.
290	- **Done:** for each word cancer: one authoritative wording at one site,
291	  citations elsewhere, the variant wordings gone.
292	
293	### 🔍 ACGm_106: ornament drain
294	
295	- **Detect:** spec sentences in normative position that neither constrain nor
296	  carry marked rationale or gestalt — prose an implementer or validator could
297	  delete without acting differently.
298	- **Authority:** the normative-register rule (the statements clause, above).
299	- **Licensing:** detect-only — whether a sentence constrains is judgment;
300	  draining is per-document and reviewed.
301	- **Verifier:** none in v1.
302	- **Done:** the spec reads as constraint plus marked rationale; deleted ornament
303	  is gone, demoted ornament is marked.
304	
305	### 🔍 ACGm_107: tidemark drain
306	
307	- **Detect:** version commentary in a durable document — revision markers, dated
308	  change narration, "new in vX" annotations. Durable means spec, guide, code, or
309	  context file; a dated artifact (memo, scar, commit message) is exempt — history
310	  is its content.
311	- **Authority:** the history-time row of the three-homes table; the concept home
312	  is MCM's `mcm_tidemark` quoin. The commit is the home — git log and blame carry
313	  the change story with a forcing function the document body lacks.
314	- **Licensing:** detect-only as a sweep; an individual drain is mutate-now when
315	  the deleted text is pure narration (the content survives in git), reviewed when
316	  narration is entangled with normative statements.
317	- **Verifier:** none in v1; candidate for a cheap regex pass in the future linter
318	  (date-shaped tokens and version markers outside memo paths).
319	- **Done:** durable documents read in the present tense; history questions answer
320	  through git.
321	
322	### 🔧 ACGm_108: bare wire-key → minted sprue
323	
324	- **Detect:** an RB-authored JSON wire format (both sender and receiver ours)
325	  whose keys are bare terms (`name`, `digest`, `stamp`) rather than sprue-carried
326	  minted names.
327	- **Authority:** the fourth clause (wire formats are named homes). The sprue
328	  itself is governed by the mint discipline (CLAUDE.md): one sprue per format,
329	  terminal-exclusive, used for nothing else.
330	- **Licensing:** mutate-now when the format's author and every consumer convert
331	  in one move and a live verifier covers the path — a rename either resolves
332	  everywhere or the suite fails loud. Detect-only when the consumers cannot all
333	  convert together; for an RB-authored format that inability is itself a finding
334	  worth recording.
335	- **Verifier:** the consuming suite tier for the format (e.g. the capture
336	  fixtures for the Lode vouch envelope), plus the census check: `grep <sprue>_`
337	  returns exactly the format's sites and nothing else.
338	- **Done:** every key in the format carries the sprue; the format's schema
339	  identifier (if it has one) is bumped; the sprue grep is the format's complete
340	  sender/receiver census. First worked application: the Lode vouch envelope
341	  (`rblv_`, schema `rbld-vouch-2`).
342	- **Triage:** foreign-schema documents are excluded by the Palisade boundary
343	  before any sweep. Pre-MVP formats convert with no dual-dialect reading — there
344	  is no compatibility surface; standing artifacts are re-captured or discarded,
345	  never grandfathered.
346	
347	## The AXLA/MCM interface
348	
349	### The lookahead scanning rule
350	
351	When counting a marker's lookahead in an MCM document, scan the following text and
352	**skip prose lines and blank lines; count only attribute-references (`{…}` linked
353	terms) and backtick sprues.** A dimension that counts "the Nth linked term" (e.g.
354	`axd_grouped`) counts attribute-references only — a backtick inlay or sprue is a
355	distinct token type and does not shift that count.
356	
357	### Name-identity's symbol-link dependency (deferred)
358	
359	ACGm_102 becomes mechanically decidable only when an operation voicing carries its
360	implementing symbol explicitly. The surveyed shape: add a one-`mcm_inlay`
361	lookahead to `axvo_procedure` / `axvo_method` reading the literal source symbol
362	(the MCM-correct token for a prefix-disciplined name, matching how
363	`axhems_scoped_method` already reads an implementation method name). It is an
364	*amendment* to two established voicings with many live consumers, not a fresh
365	mint, so it carries a real migration and is held for its own deliberate pace
366	rather than folded into guide authoring. Recorded here as ACGm_102's named
367	dependency; raised and deferred, not dropped.
368	
369	## Related Guides
370	
371	- **BCG** — host bash. Constant Discipline and Interface Contamination are the
372	  bash instances of the spine.
373	- **RCG** — host Rust. Constant Discipline (Beyond Strings), String Boundary
374	  Discipline, and Interface Contamination are the Rust instances.
375	- **WSG**, **CBG** — foreign-environment siblings (Windows transport, cloud step).
376	- **MCM** — builds the named homes ACG references; the design-time home.
377	- **AXLA** — the motif vocabulary; the lookahead scanning rule and the
378	  name-identity symbol-link dependency live against it.
379	- **CLAUDE.md "Prefix Naming Discipline" (mint)** — governs the naming homes
380	  themselves.
381	
382	## Acronym Registry
383	
384	| Term | Expansion |
385	|------|-----------|
386	| ACG | Allocation Coding Guide (this document) |
387	| The spine | "Reference the home" — values → constants, concepts → quoin-refs, statements → definition sites |
388	| Wire format | An RB-authored JSON document with senders and receivers; a named home whose keys are minted (sprued) names — the fourth clause |
389	| Word cancer | Variations of one normative idea scattered as paraphrases, each drifting independently; the metastatic violation of statements → definition sites |
390	| Normative register | Spec-side rule: every sentence constrains or is marked rationale/gestalt; unmarked ornament is contamination |
391	| Tidemark | Version commentary stranded in a durable document; history-time knowledge whose home is the commit. Quoin home: MCM `mcm_tidemark` |
392	| Home | The one right location for a fact, fixed by when it is read (design / edit / execution time) |
393	| Move-type | A catalogued `ACGm_` cleanup move: detect-rule, authority, licensing, verifier, countable Done |
394	| mutate-now | Licensing: a wrong move is cheaply caught, so the verb may repair in place |
395	| detect-only | Licensing: the verb reads and reports; no repair until a verifier exists |
396	| Name-identity | Concept linkage where the implementing symbol *is* the link to its quoin |
397

# Kind name comes from the envelope's own kind field — the kind-agnostic source
  # (a new Lode kind needs no change here; the prefix letter is shown as a cross-check).
  local z_trust=""
  z_trust=$(jq -r '.trust_grade // "(absent)"' "${z_vouch_json}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read trust_grade from envelope for ${z_touchmark}"

  echo ""
  printf "  Provenance envelope (:%s):\n" "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}"
  jq -r '
    "    Kind:          \(.kind // "(absent)")",
    "    Lode:          \(.lode // "(absent)")",
    "    Schema:        \(.schema // "(absent)")",
    "    Acquired at:   \(.acquired_at // "(absent)")",
    "    Acquired by:   \(.acquired_by // "(absent)")",
    "    Capture build: \(.capture_build // "(absent)")",
    "    Signature:     \(if .signature == null then "(unsigned)" else .signature end)",
    "    Trust grade:   \(.trust_grade // "(absent)")",
    "",
    "    Members (\((.members // []) | length)):",
    ((.members // [])[] |
      "      \(.name // "(unnamed)")",
      "        origin:       \(.origin // "(absent)")",
      "        digest:       \(.digest // "(absent)")",
      "        verification: \(.verification // "(absent)")",
      "        tags:         \((.tags // []) | join(", "))")
  ' "${z_vouch_json}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to render provenance envelope for ${z_touchmark}"
